title: Gump Roast

jokes.text: Hold it right there, Forrest Plump!
jokes.setup: Homer offering chocolate like Forrest Gump
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's fat-shaming pun on 'Forrest Gump'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum-style malapropism that's both a clever pun and character-specific incompetence. High quotability.

jokes.text: Oh, behave!
jokes.setup: Wiggum just arrested Homer for impersonating movie characters
jokes.punchline: Wiggum immediately does an Austin Powers impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocritical behavior from Wiggum - arrests Homer then immediately does what he arrested him for. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Luke, I am your father / Shagadellic
jokes.setup: Wiggum's hypocrisy established
jokes.punchline: He continues doing more impressions
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the hypocrisy gag but diminishing returns on the concept.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey. I didn't ask for your life story.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions waiting for his wife with a surprise
jokes.punchline: Wiggum cuts him off for oversharing a single sentence
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum overreaction to minimal information, perfectly in character and well-timed.

jokes.text: Things started out great. I ate what my mother ate.
jokes.setup: Homer begins his life story
jokes.punchline: His first life milestone is eating what his mother ate (in the womb)
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist take on life beginning in utero - unexpectedly literal interpretation.

jokes.text: Let go! Let go!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his mother loved chili while pregnant
jokes.punchline: Baby Homer trying to escape the womb from spicy food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist visual of conscious baby Homer reacting to spicy food in utero. Classic Simpsons surreal humor.

jokes.text: Hey, Pete! Pete! They fixed the Coke machine!
jokes.setup: Wiggum mentions undercover cop infiltrating the mob
jokes.punchline: Immediately blows the cop's cover by yelling his name in public
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - completely defeats the purpose of 'undercover' work. The mundane Coke machine detail makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Jack and Jill went up the hill and... Jill came tumbling after. The end.
jokes.setup: Young Homer expecting a bedtime story from his father
jokes.punchline: Abe delivers the most abbreviated, wrong version possible
jokes.explanation: Classic Grandpa Simpson half-effort parenting, missing key story elements and rushing to finish.

jokes.text: Say, how about a little NyQuil?
jokes.setup: Homer asking questions about Jack stories
jokes.punchline: Abe's solution is to drug his child to sleep
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny parenting shortcut that's both terrible and believable for Abe's character.

jokes.text: Why do birds suddenly appear / Every time you are near?
jokes.setup: Homer meeting Marge for the first time
jokes.punchline: Homer literally singing 'Close to You' while approaching her
jokes.explanation: Straightforward romantic comedy trope execution, nothing particularly clever but serviceable.

jokes.text: She's mine!
jokes.setup: Another man approaching Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate possessive territorial response
jokes.explanation: Basic Homer jealousy/possessiveness, predictable but in character.

jokes.text: You just won yourselves a motorcycle! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Host criticizing reckless disregard for wife's well-being
jokes.punchline: Immediately rewards them with something even more dangerous
jokes.explanation: Sharp irony - condemning dangerous behavior by giving an even more dangerous prize. Good structural misdirection.

jokes.text: You know, all this danger is kind of a turn... Okay.
jokes.setup: Marge getting excited by dangerous situations
jokes.punchline: Cuts herself off before admitting sexual arousal
jokes.explanation: Great subversive moment for Marge's character - revealing a hidden wild side then immediately suppressing it.

jokes.text: The day we got our elephant.
jokes.setup: Homer building up 'the day that changes every couple forever'
jokes.punchline: Instead of having a baby, they got an elephant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that subverts the expected 'having a baby' reveal with absurd Simpsons continuity. Perfect economy and surprise.

jokes.text: While I wait for my family, I'll tell you about the time my baby shot my boss.
jokes.setup: Homer casually transitioning to another story
jokes.punchline: Mentions baby shooting boss as casual anecdote
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual delivery of an insane event. The matter-of-fact tone makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, that's great, but I gotta get going. / Whoa! There's a whole 'nother row of these bad boys!
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to leave Homer's stories
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by more snacks
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum characterization - easily distracted by food despite having somewhere to be.

jokes.text: Get off the road, you freaking maniac! / Yeah, you jackass!
jokes.setup: Homer driving badly
jokes.punchline: Other drivers yelling at him while he's being driven
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage gag, nothing particularly creative or character-specific.

jokes.text: What? I can see fine.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting she should drive
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming he can see fine while clearly driving terribly
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to his own incompetence, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: That had nothing to do with the bucket.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashing due to bucket on windshield
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete denial of obvious cause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer denial of reality. The absurd specificity of 'the bucket' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: All my other senses are getting sharper. / Bart, you had pizza for lunch. / Lisa, you're extremely depressed. / As if.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming enhanced senses from blindfold
jokes.punchline: Correctly identifies pizza smell but brutally calls out Lisa's depression
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from mundane observation to devastating emotional insight. Lisa's weak denial adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Kathleen Turner! / No! It's me, Krusty!
jokes.setup: Krusty asking if Homer remembers his voice
jokes.punchline: Homer guesses completely wrong celebrity
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Homer's poor memory/recognition, but fairly standard.

jokes.text: Are the proceeds going to charity? / Hell, no! / Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about charitable nature of roast
jokes.punchline: Homer excited that money isn't going to charity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - celebrating that charity won't benefit. Dark but character-perfect moment.

jokes.text: We're all here tonight for one reason. / To keep Homer away from the buffet!
jokes.setup: Standard roast opening about gathering for one reason
jokes.punchline: The reason is to prevent Homer from eating all the food
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of roast format expectations with Homer-specific humor about his appetite.

jokes.text: Who's Mark Spitz? / What's a telegram? / Oh, forget it!
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to read telegrams from celebrities
jokes.punchline: Kids don't know outdated references, Krusty gives up
jokes.explanation: Good generation gap humor about dated cultural references and obsolete technology.

jokes.text: Joy to the world, the Lord is come! / Season's greetings. Peace out.
jokes.setup: Traditional Christmas carol performance
jokes.punchline: Bart's modern, casual sign-off after religious song
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between formal religious performance and Bart's casual modern attitude.

jokes.text: Oh, that hurts!
jokes.setup: Kids singing gentle Christmas carol
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow injured by peaceful song
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment, unclear setup makes it less effective.

jokes.text: Will you take us to Mount Splashmore? No. (repeated)
jokes.setup: Kids repeatedly asking to go to water park
jokes.punchline: Endless repetitive pestering until Homer breaks
jokes.explanation: Realistic parent/child dynamic escalation, relatable but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Will you take us to Mount Splashmore? Yes! / Thanks, Dad. Thanks, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer finally agrees to stop the pestering
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately continue asking the same question
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing kids' inability to stop even when they get what they want.

jokes.text: Isn't this that cartoon that causes seizures?
jokes.setup: Kids watching TV
jokes.punchline: Homer casually mentioning dangerous cartoon while kids watch
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-reference to real-world cartoon controversy with Homer's typical casual attitude toward danger.

jokes.text: Max Power, he's the man whose name you'd love to touch / But you mustn't touch
jokes.setup: Homer singing about his fake name
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical song about touching someone's name
jokes.explanation: Weird absurdist song with memorable lyrics about an untouchable name.

jokes.text: I like pizza, I like bagels / I like hot dogs with mustard and beer
jokes.setup: Homer singing about food preferences
jokes.punchline: Extremely basic, obvious food likes delivered as if profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer simplicity but not particularly clever or funny.

jokes.text: I could even eat a baby deer
jokes.setup: Homer listing foods he'd eat
jokes.punchline: Escalates to eating cute baby animal
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation that's shocking but believable for Homer's character.

jokes.text: I'm kinda like Jesus, but not in a sacrilegious way
jokes.setup: Homer comparing himself to religious figures
jokes.punchline: Claims not to be sacrilegious while being completely sacrilegious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - being blasphemous while trying to avoid being blasphemous. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Just look at all of his catastrophic nincompoopery. / Poop.
jokes.setup: Burns using elaborate vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Audience member only hears 'poop' in 'nincompoopery'
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that contrasts Burns' sophistication with audience immaturity.

jokes.text: Well, I'm sorry to disappoint you gentlemen, but you seem to have me confused with a character in a fictional show.
jokes.setup: Burns denying responsibility for Homer's disasters
jokes.punchline: Burns breaks the fourth wall by referencing being in a fictional show
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character for Burns' arrogance.

jokes.text: Carbon dating!
jokes.setup: Introducing couple that's been dating
jokes.punchline: Pun on carbon dating for very old people
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works with the elderly couple setup, good economy.

jokes.text: Sweet Toledo! What's keeping that dress on? / The collective will of everyone in this room!
jokes.setup: Grandpa making crude comment about Agnes's dress
jokes.punchline: Agnes saying everyone's will is preventing her from undressing
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback with philosophical implications about collective social restraint. Sharp and unexpected.

jokes.text: You fruits wouldn't know what to do with me!
jokes.setup: Agnes responding to crowd's rejection
jokes.punchline: Agnes calling them 'fruits' and claiming sexual superiority
jokes.explanation: Bold character moment for Agnes but the slur dates it poorly.

jokes.text: Nothing at all. Nothing at all. / Stupid sexy Flanders!
jokes.setup: Flanders in tight ski outfit
jokes.punchline: Homer's conflicted attraction despite hating Flanders
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment that became a meme. Perfect character conflict and incredibly quotable. Near-perfect comedy.

jokes.text: Ow! My legs! This is the worst pain ever!
jokes.setup: Homer doing some physical activity
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic overreaction to mild discomfort
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer physical comedy, in character but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Oh, dear! You have ruined my work, you flying fat man!
jokes.setup: Homer parasailing crashes into something
jokes.punchline: Apu's polite but direct insult about Homer's weight and flying
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reaction from Apu, polite language contrasting with direct insult.

jokes.text: I want to sneer at God's creation and spit on his... / Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer getting increasingly megalomaniacal
jokes.punchline: Gets immediately punished mid-blasphemy
jokes.explanation: Perfect divine comedy timing - immediate cosmic punishment for hubris. Great escalation and payoff.

jokes.text: Hey, Abe, you want to hook up after the show? / Yeah! To a suicide machine!
jokes.setup: Agnes asking Abe for romantic encounter
jokes.punchline: Abe wanting to kill himself instead
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective misdirection showing Abe's preference for death over romance with Agnes.

jokes.text: I'm sick of your lies! Secrets and lies!
jokes.setup: Homer getting upset at the roast
jokes.punchline: Homer dramatically overreacting with soap opera intensity
jokes.explanation: Good Homer emotional overreaction but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: The Camptown ladies sing this song / Homer Simpson's breath is strong
jokes.setup: Traditional song opening
jokes.punchline: Twisted lyrics about Homer's bad breath
jokes.explanation: Competent parody lyrics but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Our planet has been observing your puny species since your planet was created 5,000 years ago by God.
jokes.setup: Aliens explaining their observation of Earth
jokes.punchline: Mixing scientific observation with young-Earth creationism
jokes.explanation: Clever satire mixing scientific aliens with religious fundamentalism in absurd combination.

jokes.text: Because he is the fat, selfish epitome of modern man! / Hey, he stole my bit!
jokes.setup: Alien explaining why Homer represents humanity
jokes.punchline: Krusty complaining that the alien stole his roast material
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about roast comedy while maintaining the alien invasion plot. Good character moment for Krusty.

jokes.text: Le grill! / What the hell is that?
jokes.setup: Homer reading French instructions
jokes.punchline: Homer confused by French word for grill
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer language confusion moment. Simple but effective and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Why? Why must life be so hard? / Why must I fail in every attempt at masonry?
jokes.setup: Homer having trouble with BBQ construction
jokes.punchline: Homer's existential crisis over minor DIY failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's tendency to catastrophize minor setbacks.

jokes.text: These are not tears! We are vomiting from our eyes!
jokes.setup: Aliens appearing to cry at Maggie's memories
jokes.punchline: Aliens' ridiculous denial/explanation of their emotional reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd alien logic trying to maintain superiority while clearly being moved. Very quotable.

jokes.text: They'll never stop the Simpsons / Have no fears / We've got stories for years
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire summary of past episodes
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of the show's longevity and story potential
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on the show's sustainability and formula. Self-aware and catchy.

jokes.text: Sorry for the clip show / Have no fears / We've got stories for years
jokes.setup: Acknowledging this is a clip show episode
jokes.punchline: Direct apology to audience while promising better content ahead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-acknowledgment of clip show format while reassuring fans. Very self-aware and honest.

tmdb_overview: In this clip show episode, Homer is honored at a Friars' Club Roast. A number of characters show up to roast him, but the celebrating is interrupted by Kang and Kodos, who say that humanity will be judged based on Homer's experiences.
